channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Brotherhood Mutual Names Scott Figgins Integration Officer
item.description: (Image source: Brother Hood Mutual webpage.) Brotherhood Mutual Insurance Company (Fort Wayne, Ind.) has promoted Scott Figgins to Senior Vice President-Integration Officer, a role focused on coordinating operations, underwriting and systems across the organization. Scott Figgins, SVP, Integration Officer, Brotherhood Mutual. Brotherhood Mutual says the changes support alignment across underwriting, operations and enterprise functions.

item.title: FEMA Tells Court It’s Offering Jobs to Employees Let Go in January
item.description: District Court Friday evening that the Federal Emergency Management Agency has begun offering new appointments to disaster workers whose contracts the agency did not renew in January, reversing a controversial decision that prompted a coalition of labor unions, scientific groups and local governments to sue the administration. FEMA did not immediately respond to questions Friday about the court notice or how many employees received offers to return. FEMA has “initiated contact to offer new appointments” to term-limited staff whose contracts expired the first three weeks of January, U.S.

item.title: Trump Says US to Start Guiding Trapped Ships Through Hormuz
item.description: will begin guiding some neutral ships trapped in the Persian Gulf out through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday. Central Command said Sunday that it would provide military support to restore commercial shipping through Hormuz, including the use of guided-missile destroyers, aircraft and drones. Oil steadied as traders doubted the effectiveness of Trump’s plan for Hormuz, while a tanker was reportedly hit in the waterway.

item.title: California Taking Action Against State Farm Over LA Wildfire Claims
item.description: The California Department of Insurance is taking action against State Farm over an investigation that reportedly shows the carrier has been mishandling insurance claims from the January 2025 Los Angeles wildfires. Claims professionals, agents, and support teams have worked long hours – often away from their own families – to inspect losses, answer questions, issue payments, support temporary living arrangements, and help customers navigate one of the most severe wildfire events in California history,” Dan Krause, president of State Farm said in the statement. Related: Public Interest Groups Backing California Homeowners Insurance Bills According to the CDI, State Farm policyholders filed roughly 11,300 residential claims related to the L.A.

item.title: New Study Suggests Federal Action to Cut Rising Insurance Costs
item.description: Insurance companies say they have hiked premiums because of rising home and auto costs and the expenses of fixing them. A new analysis suggests Americans are being overcharged by $150 billion annually to insure their homes, autos, and businesses — and it proposes federal guardrails so that a public beset by affordability pressures could see savings. The insurance industry said its current loss ratio reflects the costs insurers have incurred in recent years and the steps deemed necessary to ensure that insurance funding remains stable and solvent.

item.title: Trump Administration Targets Dismantling of Already-Weakened DEI
item.description: In his February State of the Union address, Trump declared that his administration “ended DEI in America.” And a month later, legal strategist Edward Blum who led the successful effort to ban affirmative action in college admissions said he was no longer able to find evidence of minority-only internships at the country’s law firms despite an ongoing “hunt.” The Trump administration isn’t done, and officials have been explicit that they’re looking at situations where they believe White men, US citizens and Christians have been put at a disadvantage. “Chair Lucas and the Trump Administration are ensuring all Americans are treated fairly by rigorously enforcing civil rights laws, ending illegal DEI-motivated race and sex discrimination, and upholding the Constitution,” White House spokeswoman Liz Huston said in a statement. Casual observers might have concluded that the push to eradicate DEI would wind down after victories by self-described “anti-woke” activists and the Trump White House in 2025.

item.title: Extreme Temps Reduce Hybrid, EV Efficiency: AAA
item.description: Researchers tested six vehicles at cold, moderate, and hot temperatures to examine how electrified powertrains perform under non-ideal conditions and found that extreme temperatures reduce efficiency, range, and increase operating costs for electric vehicles (EVs) and hybrids. New research indicates cold and hot weather can significantly reduce efficiency and increase operating expenses, according to a recently released AAA study. An AAA automotive engineering team, in partnership with the Automobile Club of Southern California’s Automotive Research Center, evaluated the powertrains of three EVs and three hybrids using a a chassis dynamometer — a device like a treadmill for cars — with the HVAC (Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning) set at 72 degrees in the vehicles and the temperatures in the laboratory test cell at 20°F, 75°F, and 95°F.

item.title: AIG elevates Hong Kong duo to casualty lead roles
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Zurich Malaysia names Wayne Leow as country head for commercial insurance
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: USAA secures its largest cat bond sponsorship ever, $825m Residential Re 2026-1
item.description: USAA, the military mutual insurer, has now successfully priced its largest catastrophe bond sponsorship ever at attractive spreads, with the Residential Reinsurance 2026 Limited (Series 2026-1) cat bond now set to provide the company a meaningful $825 million in additional catastrophe reinsurance limit, Artemis can report. Which means the Residential Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond will become USAA’s largest cat bond sponsorship ever. USAA sponsored its first catastrophe bond way back in 1997, with that first Residential Re deal becoming only the second Rule 144A cat bond transaction we had ever seen.

item.title: Kansas Man Sentenced for Insurance Fraud
item.description: Kansas Insurance Commissioner Vicki Schmidt announced a Wyandotte County man has been sentenced to 16 months in prison for insurance fraud. Christopher Dunn, aged 33, pleaded guilty on March 2, 2026, in Wyandotte County District Court to one felony count of insurance fraud. The Kansas Department of Insurance investigated this case which was prosecuted by the Kansas Attorney General’s Office.

item.title: Massive Explosion at NYC Home Sends Police Officers Flying
item.description: A massive, fiery explosion at a house in Queens sent several New York City police officers flying through the air early last Thursday morning as they were responding to a call of a man inside armed with a knife and the smell of gas. Police body camera video of the explosion shows officers about to enter a small home in Queens before 3 a.m., and just as one starts to open the door, a huge blast erupts, blowing out windows and knocking officers back across a yard and into a gate. Eight officers were treated for minor injuries — including burns and at least one head laceration — after the blast, and all of the people who were inside the building have been accounted for, though several were taken to a hospital, police said.

item.title: American Farmers Shun USDA Surveys as Trust in Data Erodes
item.description: American farmers are ignoring US Department of Agriculture surveys in the latest sign of distrust in government data. The mounting skepticism comes as the USDA is looking to become more responsive to farmers through a transformation that relies heavily on shared data. Farmers say they are wary of sharing data to inform reports they feel often work against them, especially with abundant harvests in recent years keeping crop prices under pressure.

item.title: WTW outlines AI strategy following Newfront acquisition, focuses on scale and defensibility
item.description: The post WTW outlines AI strategy following Newfront acquisition, focuses on scale and defensibility appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Willis Towers Watson (WTW) has detailed its artificial intelligence strategy following its acquisition of Newfront, positioning AI as a long-term driver of growth, efficiency, and competitive advantage rather than a tool for simple cost reduction. Looking ahead, WTW plans to scale Newfront’s agentic AI capabilities across the organisation and deepen adoption among employees, creating a compounding effect as usage grows and models improve.

item.title: Willis launches Merger Protect for M&A regulatory costs
item.description: Willis, a WTW business, has introduced Merger Protect, an insurance solution aimed at covering costs tied to U.S. Merger Protect gives deal parties something they haven’t had before: a way to protect against the cost volatility of regulatory review without compromising their ability to defend the transaction. Merger Protect converts those variable expenses into a predefined insurance cost, helping deal parties manage uncertainty and preserve deal economics.

item.title: Unum names Steve Jones president of Colonial Life
item.description: Unum has named Steve Jones as president of Colonial Life , effective June 8, as part of a planned leadership transition. Jones joined Colonial Life in 2023 as senior vice president of field and market development. McKenney said Colonial Life remains central to Unum’s growth strategy and pointed to Jones’ background in driving results and working with employers and brokers.

item.title: Trucordia acquires Paradiso Insurance
item.description: Trucordia has acquired Paradiso Financial and Insurance Services , a Stafford Springs-based agency serving individuals, families, and businesses. Paradiso offers a range of coverage across personal and commercial lines, including auto, home, life, and specialty insurance, with a focus on simplifying insurance decisions for clients. The deal expands Trucordia’s presence in Connecticut and New England, while giving Paradiso access to additional carrier relationships and national resources.

item.title: La competencia también alimenta el mercado de datos robados
item.description: Y en industrias intensivas en datos, la posibilidad de que algunas compañías estén dispuestas a pagar por la información para obtener ventajas en el mercado empieza a tensionar la relación entre los competidores. “A veces las contrapartes que están dispuestas a pagar por esos datos no son personas maliciosas, sino que puede ser la competencia. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: What Every Claims Leader Needs to Know as a First-Time Buyer
item.description: In light of this adoption, Wisedocs has released our exclusive Buyer’s Guide to help claims leaders confidently evaluate AI-powered Claims Documentation Platforms (CDPs) and choose a solution that fits their unique needs. View Series Claims professionals know better than anyone: the paperwork never stops. Choosing the right claims documentation platform (CDP) matters, and so does finding one that fits your needs.

item.title: AIG Underwriting Income More Than Triples in Q1
item.description: First-quarter 2026 underwriting income in American International Group’s (AIG) General Insurance segment increased nearly 220% compared with the prior year, to $774 million. Underwriting income in North America commercial was $337 million for Q1, compared with $129 million the prior year. Global personal reversed a Q1 2025 underwriting loss of $126 million to record underwriting income of $169 million for Q1 2026.

item.title: Iran War Is ‘Supercharging’ Clean Energy Transition, UN Climate Chief Says
item.description: The Iran war is “supercharging” the world’s shift to renewable energy, as countries scramble to reduce their exposure to volatile oil and gas markets, the U.N. “The best way to protect citizens from the violent convulsions of global energy markets is to accelerate the clean-energy transition,” he said in a statement after the IEA event. “Those who’ve fought to keep the world hooked on fossil fuels are inadvertently supercharging the global renewables boom,” said Simon Stiell, Executive Secretary of the U.N.’s climate secretariat UNFCCC.

item.title: US Navy escorts, securing terms key to insuring Strait of Hormuz transits: Berkshire’s Ajit Jain
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: SCOR returns with $75m initial target for Atlas Capital 2026-1 multi-peril cat bond
item.description: SCOR, the France-headquartered global reinsurance company, is back in the catastrophe bond market initially targeting $75 million of multi-peril fully-collateralized retrocession through sponsorship of an Atlas Capital DAC (Series 2026-1) issuance, Artemis has learned. This new Atlas Capital DAC Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond will become the twentieth from SCOR to use the Atlas name and the twenty-first cat bond from the company that we have tracked since the year 2000, including its Horizon securitization of credit liabilities back in 2001. For this Atlas Capital 2026 cat bond, SCOR will once again use its Ireland-based designated activity company named Atlas Capital DAC, now making it four cat bond sponsorships through this structure.

item.title: Mapfre Re finalises $200m Recoletos Re 2026-1 cat bond, at low-end pricing
item.description: Mapfre Re, the Spanish global reinsurance company, has now successfully secured $200 million of US named storm protection from the capital markets through its Recoletos Re DAC (Series 2026-1) catastrophe bond issuance, pricing the notes at the lowest end of initial guidance, Artemis can report. With these new cat bond notes now priced and the coverage secured once the deal settles, Recoletos Re DAC, Mapfre Re’s Ireland-based issuance vehicle, will issue a $200 million single tranche of Series 2026-1 Class A notes, that will be sold to cat bond investors and the proceeds be used to collateralize a retrocession agreement between the issuing vehicle and Mapfre Re. Mapfre Re made its return to the catastrophe bond market in April, with the third in the series of Recoletos Re DAC cat bonds.

item.title: FEMA Tells Court It’s Offering Jobs Back to Employees Let Go in January
item.description: District Court Friday evening that the Federal Emergency Management Agency has begun offering new appointments to disaster workers whose contracts the agency did not renew in January, reversing a controversial decision that prompted a coalition of labor unions, scientific groups and local governments to sue the administration. FEMA did not immediately respond to questions Friday about the court notice or how many employees received offers to return. FEMA has “initiated contact to offer new appointments” to term-limited staff whose contracts expired the first three weeks of January, U.S.

item.title: Hedge Fund Fermat Invests in First-of-a-Kind Catastrophe Bonds
item.description: Fermat Capital Management has invested in the Asian Development Bank’s first-ever catastrophe bonds, designed to shield Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan against specific types of natural disasters. “Fermat is generally called upon to be the lead investor in first-of-its-kind deals such as this one,” a spokesperson for the Connecticut-based firm said in an emailed response to questions, confirming it had participated in the ADB bond sale. The hedge fund manager, which has been investing in cat bonds for more than 20 years, said the ADB’s governance mechanism allows “for the transparent, accountable disbursement of bond proceeds in the rare event that a payout is triggered,” the spokesperson said.

item.title: United Jet Hits Truck and Light Pole on Turnpike in Landing at Newark Airport
item.description: A United Airlines aircraft on Sunday struck a light pole and a trailer during its final approach to Newark Liberty International Airport, the New Jersey State Police said. The underside and tire of United Airlines Flight 169 struck a light pole and tractor-trailer on the New Jersey Turnpike around 2 p.m., according to a New Jersey State Police spokesperson. “Our maintenance team is evaluating damage to the aircraft,” said a United spokesperson.

item.title: InsureMO expands ecosystem with Upsure core suite to streamline insurer modernisation
item.description: The post InsureMO expands ecosystem with Upsure core suite to streamline insurer modernisation appeared first on Insurtech Insights . By combining InsureMO’s connectivity, AI, and product configuration layer with Upsure’s configurable core suite, carriers can move from vendor selection to deployment through a more unified and coordinated approach. Our core suite is built by insurance practitioners; pairing it with InsureMO’s platform means carriers can move from selection to live deployment without the usual multi-vendor integration tax.” The addition of Upsure is part of InsureMO’s ongoing expansion of its Partner Apps directory, which now includes core systems, AI tools, and specialist solutions for underwriting, claims, and fraud detection.

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway Q1 profit surges as operating earnings rise 18%
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway reported a significant increase in first-quarter 2026 profit, with strong operating performance outweighing investment-related volatility driven by US accounting rules. Across non-insurance operations, BNSF Railway generated $1.38 billion in earnings, up from $1.21 billion, while Berkshire Hathaway Energy contributed $1.11 billion, broadly in line with the prior year. Operating earnings, which exclude unrealized investment gains and losses, grew 18% year-on-year to $11.3 billion from $9.6 billion, reflecting stronger contributions from the group’s core businesses.

item.title: GEICO ends Q1 with $1.4 billion profit
item.description: GEICO ended the first quarter of 2026 with a $1.4 billion underwriting profit, a ~34% decrease ($757M) compared to the same period last year. Premiums written increased $168 million (1.5%) in the first quarter to $11.7 billion, reflecting an increase in commercial auto business, partially offset by lower average premiums per policy for private passenger auto insurance. GEICO’s loss ratio (losses and loss adjustment expenses to premiums earned) was 73.9% in the first quarter of 2026, an increase of 4.9 percentage points compared to 2025.

item.title: Slide expands coverage to California
item.description: Slide Insurance announced its expansion into California, launching a residential property excess and surplus lines program and writing its first policy in the state. California is the third market for Slide, which is also available in Florida and South Carolina. California homeowners and landlords deserve reliable, customized insurance solutions, and our underwriting expertise and financial strength position us to deliver that stability.” – Bruce Lucas, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Slide.

item.title: Ebix introduces AI-powered COI compliance platform
item.description: Ebix has launched a new version of its certificate of insurance (COI) management platform, adding AI-driven validation and workflow capabilities aimed at closing compliance gaps. The platform also introduces self-service workflows for in-house compliance teams. This creates a larger growth opportunity for Ebix in the risk and compliance market.” – Gagan Sethi, CEO of Ebix Technologies.

item.title: SSR Iberia comienza una nueva etapa bajo la dirección de Víctor Moreno
item.description: SSR Iberia anuncia oficialmente el nombramiento de Víctor Moreno como nuevo director general en el marco de una nueva etapa para la agencia. Estos cambios se producen tras más de 15 años al frente de SSR Iberia por parte de Prudencio de Luis y Lorenzo y Ángel Zabala Trueba, quienes dejan la dirección al alcanzar la edad de jubilación. Asimismo, Marisa Carrasco se incorpora como nueva directora de Marketing y Comunicación, asumiendo también funciones de coordinación de ventas y relaciones con clientes.

item.title: El dato centra la nueva gestión del riesgo en seguros
item.description: La gestión del riesgo en seguros ha entrado en una nueva fase. Uno de los mensajes más contundentes del informe es que e l riesgo en seguros es hoy, en gran medida, riesgo de datos . Así lo refleja la tercera ‘Encuesta Anual Global sobre Gestión de Riesgos en el Sector Asegurador’ ( ‘The new shape of risk’ ) de EY, realizada en colaboración con el Instituto de Finanzas Internacionales (IIF), que analiza cómo los chief risk officers (CROs) están redefiniendo su papel en un sector cada vez más dependiente de los datos y la tecnología.

item.title: La incertidumbre económica reduce las previsiones de crecimiento del Seguro
item.description: Esta revisión tiene consecuencias para las estimaciones de crecimiento del seguro en España en 2026 y 2027 . El informe ‘Panorama Económico y Sectorial 2026: actualización de previsiones hacia el segundo trimestre’ , publicado el jueves pasado, anticipa un aumento del 6% en las primas de No Vida en 2026 y del 6,6% en 2027 (crecimiento nominal anual). Mapfre Economics ha bajado su previsión de crecimiento para la economía española en 2026 hasta el 2,1%, una décima menos que en sus estimaciones previas.

item.title: Public Adjuster Charged with Stealing $140K in Insurance Payouts
item.description: Micucci was charged with nine counts each of insurance fraud, theft by deception, deceptive business practices, and misappropriation of moneys held on behalf of another. The Bucks County district attorney announced criminal charges against the owner and operator of Advanced Public Adjusters. Micucci, of Bristol, Pennsylvania, is accused of misappropriating approximately $140,000 in insurance claim funds intended for nine victims and their contractors.

item.title: UK Court Orders Samsung to Pay ZTE $392 Million Lump Sum After Patent Trial
item.description: Samsung Electronics must pay a $392 million lump sum for a license to use ZTE’s patents for mobile phones, London’s High Court ruled on Friday in the English leg of the companies’ global licensing dispute. Judge Richard Meade ruled that Samsung pay a lump sum of $392 million after the two companies were unable to agree on the renewal of a previous 2021 deal. The lump sum is more than the maximum $200 million Samsung had argued for, but less than the $731 million ZTE had sought.

item.title: Steel Company Must Spend $12M to Address Contamination at Operation
item.description: A steel producer must spend $12 million to mitigate hazardous waste violations at its Ohio facility. The corrective measures will address several site areas at the Middletown Works, a 2,600-acre integrated steel mill in operation since 1901. The Department of Justice announced a proposed settlement with Cleveland-Cliffs Steel Corporation over releases of hazardous waste at its Middletown Works operation.

item.title: Hippo ‘Off to a Fast Start’ With Reversal of Q1 Loss a Year Ago
item.description: InsurTech Hippo reversed a first-quarter net loss of $47.7 million last year and posted net income of about $7.1 million for the first three months of 2026. “We got off to a fast start in 2026, significantly advancing our strategies on both growth and operational efficiencies,” Rick McCathron, president and CEO, said in a statement. Related: Hippo, Progressive Embark on Strategic Distribution Relationship Gross written premium of $332 million during Q1 increased 58% compared with the same period a year ago.

item.title: Cost of Howden-Driven Talent War Rises to $31M for Brown & Brown
item.description: Brown & Brown acknowledged that the cost of the Howden-driven talent war has risen to $31 million in annual revenue. Brown & Brown, headquartered in Daytona Beach, Fla., previously revealed that 275 of B&B’s former teammates joined Howden’s U.S. Related: Brown & Brown Files Suit Over Alleged Howden Poaching of 200+ Employees During its Q1 2026 analysts’ call on April 28, Watts and Brown discussed the impact of property catastrophe rates on the company’s results.

item.title: Asia nat cat insured losses top US$5bn, economic damage reaches US$65bn in 2025: Swiss Re
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Australia’s ATC acquires Frontier Global to expand financial lines, London market footprint
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Sudarlin Uzir joins Aon Indonesia as head of industry specialties
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending May 3rd 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending May 3rd 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending May 3rd 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: CISA Weighs Cutting Deadlines to Fix Digital Flaws Amid Worries Over AI
item.description: cybersecurity officials are considering sharply shorter deadlines for fixing critical flaws in government IT systems, amid concerns hackers could exploit them using artificial‑intelligence tools such as Anthropic’s Mythos, people familiar with the matter said. CISA has typically given civilian agencies a three-week deadline to fix such flaws once they are added to the database, according to cybersecurity researcher Glenn Thorpe, although that has recently dropped to around two weeks. The discussions at CISA come as business leaders and the digital security industry grapple with the fallout from the release of more advanced AI models.

item.title: SpaceX Sued for Alleged Home Damage in South Texas
item.description: A group of 80 South Texas plaintiffs are suing Elon Musk’s aerospace company SpaceX, alleging its rocket testing caused “massive” sonic booms that damaged their houses repeatedly over a two-year period. SpaceX has increased its footprint in the South Texas region significantly as it has ramped up the frequency of its rocket launches and size of its operations. Southern District of Texas Court on Thursday, accusing the company of gross negligence and trespassing for loud blasts caused by 11 rocket tests from April 2023 to October 2025.

item.title: Plane Crashes in Texas Hill Country, Killing 5 Pickleball Players
item.description: Five members of a pickleball club who died after the small plane carrying them crashed in Texas are being mourned by a tight-knit community of fellow players. The Texas Department of Public Safety confirmed Saturday that Justin Appling, Hayden Dillard, Brooke Skypala, Stacy Hedrick and Seren Wilson were on board the Cessna 421C that crashed Thursday night in Texas Hill Country. They had traveled to Pro Pickleball Association-sanctioned tournaments all over the country together, from Dallas to Las Vegas.

item.title: InsureMO and DICEUS Partner to Accelerate Insurance Modernization Across Europe, Middle East, and Asia-Pacific
item.description: The post InsureMO and DICEUS Partner to Accelerate Insurance Modernization Across Europe, Middle East, and Asia-Pacific appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Singapore & London, April 30, 2026 — InsureMO , the global insurance API, Data, and AI Platform-as-a-Service, and DICEUS, the insurance technology company delivering ready-made platforms for policy administration, underwriting, and customer engagement, today announced a strategic technology partnership spanning Europe, the Middle East, and Asia-Pacific. By combining InsureMO’s API-first platform with DICEUS’s pre-built, modular software suite, the partnership gives insurers a faster path to full-stack modernization without replacing existing systems.

item.title: InsureMO Expands Partner Ecosystem with Upsure’s Core Insurance Suite
item.description: Upsure brings a modular core insurance suite covering policy administration, underwriting, claims, distribution, and policyholder servicing to InsureMO customers. With Upsure now in the InsureMO partner ecosystem, carriers in India, Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and Africa can deploy InsureMO’s connectivity, AI, and product configuration layer alongside Upsure’s configurable core through a single coordinated engagement. SINGAPORE, April 30, 2026: InsureMO , the global insurance platform spanning API, Data, AI, and Partner Apps, today welcomed Upsure (Jiva Business Ventures Pvt.

item.title: belo levanta US$ 14 M y pone en mira segmento B2B
item.description: “La compañía también está trabajando en ampliar sus casos de uso, incluyendo el desarrollo del segmento B2B, con el objetivo de llevar su propuesta a empresas y generar nuevas fuentes de crecimiento”, dijo a iupana Manuel Beaudroit, cofundador y CEO de belo. La fintech argentina belo levantó una serie A de US$ 14 millones, en una ronda liderada por Tether, uno de los principales emisores de stablecoins del mundo. El capital recaudado ayudará para el lanzamiento de nuevas líneas de negocio, como el segmento corporativo.
